text:
A timid squirrel named Charlie lived deep in the forest. Charlie loved apples. Every autumn, Charlie collected as many apples as he could carry. But Charlie hated leaving his nest. One evening, Charlie saw an elderly owl perched silently on an old oak. The owl gazed curiously at Charlie.

"You collect apples?" the owl whispered.

"Yes," Charlie answered nervously.

"Well," the owl began thoughtfully, "there's a giant apple tree just upstream. Those apples are delicious. You could carry some back."

Charlie thought about it. An adventure! Maybe he could keep them from a hawk.

With cautious steps, Charlie slid down the rope. Then the owl flew away.

Charlie brought back lots of apples. The hawk swooped. Charlie darted up. They struggled.

"You've got these," Charlie said.

The hawk took them.

Next year Charlie got more. An unusual gray bird picked them.

Some years later... Charlie couldn't remember that creature.

In old age, Charlie discovered
